panels.description: Medium shot: Mr. Bennet looks kindly at Elizabeth as she enters the room. Warm home lighting, domestic atmosphere.

bubbles.text: I am glad you are come back, Lizzy.

panels.description: Wide establishing shot: A crowded, bustling dining room. Lady Lucas, Mrs. Bennet, Jane, and Lydia are all engaged in loud, overlapping conversations. Victorian furniture and candlelight.

bubbles.text: The dining-room was a hive of news and chatter. Lady Lucas spoke of poultry, Mrs. Bennet of fashions, and Lydia of the morning's excitement.

panels.description: Medium close-up: Mary Bennet speaking primly to a distracted Elizabeth, while Lydia is visible in the background, ignoring them entirely.

bubbles.text: I should infinitely prefer a book.

panels.description: Close-up: Elizabeth's face, looking conflicted and resolute. She is thinking of Wickham. Soft focus background.

bubbles.text: Elizabeth opposed the walk to Meryton. She dreaded seeing Wickham again.

panels.description: Wide shot: Elizabeth sitting in a study, looking out a window as the sun sets, feeling a sense of relief about the regiment leaving, while shadows of her parents discussing 'Brighton' loom in the doorway behind her.

bubbles.text: The removal of the regiment was a blessing. But the Brighton scheme was already being whispered.
